patches.summary: Align CLI, bundled MCP, User-Agent, and GoReleaser version surfaces with release 1.1.2.
patches.reason: The installed Go module was v1.1.2, but --version and agent-context still reported 1.0.0 because direct go-install builds do not inject release ldflags and the GoReleaser ldflag path was stale.

patches.summary: Align generated CLI commands and spec with live body contracts for contact tags, invoices, notifications, and automations.
patches.reason: The generated CLI spec had drifted from the live API validators: contact tag add required tagIds[], invoice create requires items, notification userId is optional with push/url supported, and automation kind defaults to native.

patches.summary: Remove stale static tool manifest and point the repo skill at the maintained CLI install path.
patches.reason: Runtime discovery now comes from the Cobra command tree via agent-context; the old static tools-manifest.json still advertised stale contracts and the skill pointed at the legacy Printing Press tree.

patches.summary: Flatten the API's nested {data:{data:[],meta}} read envelope and expose the payload under the canonical "data" key (with "results" kept as a back-compat alias) across all read commands.
patches.reason: Dogfood v1.1.3: deals list --pipeline appeared to return 0 rows because the raw nested envelope was wrapped again, burying items at results.data.data[]. Generator-level fix in the shared wrapWithProvenance so every read command (70+ resources) flattens to a single predictable depth and standardizes the payload key with the mutation envelope.
patches.validated_outcome: deals list --pipeline <id> returns rows at envelope.data[]; pagination meta surfaced at envelope.meta.pagination; HIRING pipeline filter correctly returns 0/total:0 while MKT returns 146669.

patches.summary: Teach paginatedGet to unwrap nested data.data items and walk every page using the pagination meta (page/per_page/total) for offset-paginated APIs; route all progress/completion events to a centralized stderr writer.
patches.reason: Dogfood v1.1.3: deals list --all reported total:0/pages:1 (extractPaginatedItems only looked one level deep and there was no meta-driven page walk). Progress events also needed a hard stdout/stderr guarantee so JSON on stdout is never interleaved.
patches.validated_outcome: deals list --all --stage <30-deal stage> --per-page 10 walks 3 pages, collects 30 rows, emits complete{total:30,pages:3} on stderr, stdout parses as a single clean JSON document with zero interleaved progress lines.

patches.summary: Mask request body values in --dry-run output by default (keys preserved for shape verification); add a --show-body flag to reveal the verbatim body for debugging.
patches.reason: Dogfood v1.1.3 (MED): dry-run printed full request bodies to stderr, which can carry finance amounts, customer PII, and notes into terminal scrollback and CI logs.
patches.validated_outcome: deals create --dry-run prints values as type markers («string»/«number»); --show-body reveals real values.

patches.summary: Accept --pipeline-id as a hidden alias of --pipeline on deals list, matching deals create's --pipeline-id flag.
patches.reason: Dogfood v1.1.3 (MED): flag naming was inconsistent — create used --pipeline-id, list used --pipeline and rejected --pipeline-id.
patches.validated_outcome: deals list --pipeline-id <id> returns the same rows as --pipeline <id>.

patches.summary: Point the doctor reachability and credential probes at /auth/me instead of the bare versioned root (which 308-redirects to a 404 route).
patches.reason: Dogfood v1.1.3 (LOW): doctor reported 'api: reachable (HTTP 404 at /)'. /auth/me is a real authenticated GET, giving a clean 'reachable' verdict and a definitive valid/invalid credential check.
patches.validated_outcome: doctor --json now reports api: reachable and credentials: valid against live prod with a valid token.
